summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the new year with a powerful call to adopt a 'whatever it takes' mentality for 2026. Using the story of the paralyzed man lowered through the roof from Mark 2, he challenged the congregation to move from being part of the crowd to being among the called. The message emphasized that a life of radical faith and obedience comes with a cost, but leads to breakthrough, healing, and God's glory. Pastor Travis shared four specific 'prices' to pay: the cost of a decisive commitment, the cost of carrying burdens for others, the cost of leaving behind the crowd that blocks access to Jesus, and the cost of tearing down the 'roofs' or limitations in our lives. He concluded with an altar call, inviting people to surrender to Jesus and experience the greatest miracle of spiritual freedom, encouraging everyone to let their lives become a trophy of God's grace.
mainPoints: A 'whatever it takes' mentality requires paying a price, including making a decisive commitment before seeing the results.
True faith involves carrying burdens for others and tearing down the obstacles that block our access to Jesus.
The ultimate reward for this mentality is not just personal breakthrough but becoming a visible testimony that brings glory to God.

versesMentioned.text: Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.'

But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.' So he said to the man, 'I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.' He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, 'We have never seen anything like this!'
Carry each other's burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Mark 2:4 - 'Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof...'
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life where you've been waiting for a sign. Make a firm, faith-filled decision to move forward in it today.
Monday.prayer: God, give me the courage to decide by faith, not by sight. My mind is made up to follow you.

Tuesday.action: Intentionally check in on one person who might be struggling. Offer a word of encouragement or practical help.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, help me be a faithful friend who carries burdens. Show me who needs support today.

Wednesday.action: Evaluate your schedule or relationships. Remove one non-essential thing that is crowding out time for prayer or scripture.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, show me what is blocking my access to you. I choose to clear the room for your presence.

Thursday.scripture: Mark 2:4 - '...they made an opening in the roof...'
Thursday.action: Name one 'roof' or limitation you've accepted (e.g., fear, an old habit). Take one concrete step to begin removing it.
Thursday.prayer: God, I am willing to tear down what holds me back. Give me strength to break through to freedom.

Friday.action: Do one thing today that visibly demonstrates your faith, whether it's sharing an encouraging word or serving someone.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, let my faith be visible today in my actions, not just my words.

Saturday.scripture: Mark 2:12 - 'He got up...walked out in full view of them all.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on a past victory or answered prayer. Share that testimony with one person to encourage their faith.
Saturday.prayer: Father, let my life be a testimony that amazes others and points them to your glory.
